title: [Synthesis][AIG] Add Synthesis dialect and change AIG dialect to a sub-dialect of synthesis
body:
Currently AIG dialect is used as core representation for circt-synth flow but for synthesis there are few other operations I'd like to introduce but not limited to AIG. From that perspective I'm thinking it's better to introduce a dialect for synthesis and move AIG to sub-dialect of Synthesis. This is my current sketch for what looks like. 

### Meta operations for synthesis 
* `synth.choice` operation -- defer lowering decisions to the late stage/actual tech mapping
```mlir
%0 = synth.choice %slow_but_small, %balance, %fast_but_large : i42
```
 inspired by https://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~alanmi/publications/2005/tech05_map.pdf but not limited to AIG node

* `synth.bundle` operation -- This is basically a compiler hint to map multiple output cells. Without this kind of operations it's necessary to recognize pairs of root operations in memory and DAG rewriting becomes much annoying to manage. 
```mlir
%carry = comb.and %a, %b: i1
%sum = comb.xor %a, %b: i1
%carry', %sum' = synth.bundle %carry, %sum : i1, i1
```
* `synth.store` operation -- rollback to original operation
```mlir
%original = synth.store (%a, %b) {
    %0 = comb.mul %a, %b : i32
   synth.store.return %0: i32
}
%tech_mapped = hw.instance @some_gate (..) {arrivatl_time=1500}
%0 = synth.choice %tech_mapped, %original : i32

==> Restore pass

%original = comb.mul %a, %b : i32
%tech_mapped = hw.instance @some_gate (...) {arrival_time=1500}
%0 = synth.choice %tech_mapped, %original : i32

==> Rerun synthesis pipeline with post mapped timing analysis
%tech_mapped = hw.instance @some_gate (...) {arrival_time=1500}
%tech_mapped_2 = hw.instance @some_gate (...) {arrival_time=1400}
%0 = synth.choice %tech_mapped, %tech_mapped_2 : i32
```


### Logic Representation 
Currently both AIG and MIG are widely used in academia and it would be beneficial to being able to experiment both representations 

* `synth.aig.and_inv` operation (renamed from `aig.and_inv`)
* `synth.mig.majority_inv` operation.  MIG has emerged as a new efficient representation for logic synthesis https://si2.epfl.ch/demichel/publications/archive/2016/07293649.pdf. 

Most of the existing passes/analysis for AIG can be easily extensible to MIG so should be ok. 
Either keeping AIG and MIG different dialects or defining it as sub-dialect of synthesis is an open question. 

### Migration
Migration is mostly mechanical:
* Synthesis dialect set up. Merge with existing lib/Synthesis directory
* Move AIG/Transforms to Synthesis/Transforms (or Synthesis/AIG/Transforms if the pass is specific to AIG)
* AIG CAPI/Python binding needs to be moved to synth
